hooks.prompt: Review the files modified in this session (check `git diff`). For each modified file: 1) Check if corresponding pytest tests exist under `tests/`. 2) If tests exist, verify they still pass. 3) If tests are missing for new logic — especially API handlers, multi-tenant scoping, or DB queries — write them. 4) Report a summary of test coverage status. If any tests fail, return {"continue": true} so the main agent can fix the issues.

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty"); if echo "$CMD" | grep -q "git commit"; then ruff check . && (npx eslint app.js 2>/dev/null || true) && (mypy server.py 2>/dev/null || true) && pytest; fi'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty"); if echo "$CMD" | grep -q "gh pr create"; then ruff check . && (npx eslint app.js 2>/dev/null || true) && (mypy server.py 2>/dev/null || true) && pytest; fi'
